reasoning: Query asks for Buddhist temple stays (宿坊). One hit (Zenkoji precinct article) is highly relevant. First result is a Chinese-language ryokan (irrelevant). Wikidata hits return historic post-town landmarks (旧名手宿本陣, 奈良井宿), not shukubo. Relevant content is buried under noise. 89 matches but few on-target.
missing_data: Named shukubo (e.g., Koyasan temples, Dewa Sanzan, Koyasan lodges) with booking info, pricing range, what experiences are offered (zazen, morning prayer, shojin ryori).
wrong_data: Chinese-language content (中斋旅馆, Zhongje Ryokan) appears as top hits — these are unrelated lodgings from an Iwate tourism site scraped in Chinese.
improvement_hint: Add category tag 'shukubo' or 'temple_lodge' to relevant attractions; re-rank so Koyasan/Dewa Sanzan/Shingon temple lodging results surface first.

reasoning: Query asks for quiet hot spring towns suitable for yukata strolling (温泉街). Top hit Yunotsu Onsen (Shimane) is excellent — old townscape, World Heritage, perfect match. But subsequent results are mountains (温泉ヶ岳, 温泉富士) and temples (温泉寺 x3). 167 matches yet relevant village-type onsen towns are buried. Agent needs to filter by settlement type.
missing_data: Classic yukuta-stroll onsen towns: Kinosaki, Gero, Yufuin, Ginzan, Shibu Onsen. Walking-distance between ryokan info, street width suitability.
improvement_hint: Add 'onsen_town' kind tag to settlement-type onsen attractions; weight prominence_score and heritage district designations to rank village onsen over mountains named 温泉.

reasoning: Query is in French asking to see whales in Japan. Tool returns クジラ results but none are whale-watching services. Top hit is a whale statue in Nagoya (monument). Other hits are completely irrelevant: Kyoto river events, Monet garden in Kochi, Ishikawa park, Okayama fruit tourism. No whale-watching spots or cetacean-related marine activity found.
missing_data: Whale-watching locations: Kerama Islands (Okinawa), Ogasawara, Kochi (Muroto), Zamami. Season info (humpbacks Jan-Apr in Okinawa), booking info.
improvement_hint: Add 'whale_watching' activity category and geo-tagged whale-watching operators to the corpus; search on ホエールウォッチング as well as クジラ for experiential queries.

reasoning: Query asks for deep tea ceremony experience, not a tourist show, in Kyoto. Tool calls get_japan_heritage(Kyoto) returning 6 Japan Heritage stories, none of which are about tea ceremony. Results cover Lake Biwa Canal, Tango chirimen silk — culturally rich but entirely off-topic. Zero relevance to 茶道 or immersive chado experience.
missing_data: Tea schools/urasenke/omotesenke, dedicated chado experience venues in Kyoto, temples offering multi-hour tea lessons, cost/booking details.
improvement_hint: For tea ceremony queries, call search_area with q='茶道' or get_traditional_arts; get_japan_heritage without theme filter returns irrelevant heritage stories when topic is experiential.

reasoning: Query asks for mountain villages served by infrequent local train lines. Tool returns ローカル results with almost no relevant content: cookie-consent page from Iwate, facility congestion page from Hiroshima, Hokkaido tourism video library, DMO policy documents, and a DMO listing. No mountain village or local-railway content found.
missing_data: Specific rural lines (Iiyama Line, Hisatsu Line, Akechi Railway), mountain village names, train frequency data, access from major cities.
wrong_data: Top results are nav-chrome pages (link policy, browser requirements) that should be filtered out during scraping.
improvement_hint: Filter nav-chrome / cookie-consent pages at scrape or index time. Add railway-related tags to stations and villages; consider a get_transport tool or indexed railway timetable data.

reasoning: Query asks for non-mainstream traditional food culture. Tool returns get_local_food with 24,262 items, capped at 500. The GI-registered items shown (Aomori Cassis, Tajima Beef, Yubari Melon, Yame Gyokuro) are well-documented and often mainstream. The constraint 'not gone mainstream' is not addressable by the server — it cannot rank by obscurity. Content is accurate and useful for food tourism generally but misses the 'lesser-known' angle.
missing_data: Ranking by obscurity or regional uniqueness. Fermented foods, regional miso varieties, narezushi — the unusual items are buried in 24K records.
improvement_hint: Add a keyword filter (e.g., q='発酵') to narrow to fermented foods; or add a 'niche_score' field inversely related to media coverage volume.

reasoning: Japanese-language version of L4-01. Same data is returned in Japanese, which is appropriate for the ja query. GI items are well-described in Japanese. Same structural issue: 'not mainstream' constraint cannot be honored; agent gets 500 of 24K unsorted records. Translation fidelity looks good (ja=ja). Minor: mainstream items like Yubari Melon are internationally famous, contradicting the query intent.
missing_data: Obscure fermented/regional foods. Keyword filter would help surface unusual items.
improvement_hint: Support keyword filters in get_local_food (e.g., q='発酵' or q='なれずし') so agents can narrow to niche categories rather than scanning all 24K records.

reasoning: Query asks for forgotten craftsman skills. Tool returns get_traditional_arts listing 183 UNESCO ICH items. Bunraku and Kabuki are world-famous, not 'forgotten'. Chakkirako (Kanagawa dance), Dainichido Bugaku (Akita shrine), Hayachine Kagura are genuinely obscure — relevant. But top items are famous arts, not dying crafts. The 'craft skill' vs 'performing art' distinction is not made; no description of endangered status.
missing_data: Designation data on which crafts have <10 practitioners, apprenticeship crises. METI craft data (get_local_specialty) would be more relevant than UNESCO ICH for 'craftsman skills'.
improvement_hint: Route 'craftsman/dying craft' queries to get_local_specialty(category='craft') rather than get_traditional_arts; add practitioner-count or 'endangered' flag to craft records.

reasoning: Query asks for Hokusai-like landscapes. Tool searches 富士 and returns excellent data: Mt. Fuji (prominence 1.0, UNESCO WH, sacred mountain), Fuji Five Lakes, Fujisan Hongu Sengen Taisha, Fuji Omuro Sengen Shrine, Fujiya Hotel, and the UNESCO WH cultural entry. Heritage labels and prominence scores are all present. Strong signal for the agent to compose a rich answer about the iconic Fuji views Hokusai depicted.
missing_data: Specific viewpoints (Chureito Pagoda, Motosuko Lake angle, Mihono Matsubara) that directly replicate Hokusai print compositions.
improvement_hint: Add Hokusai-specific location tags or link scenic viewpoint data to attraction records for famous artistic vantage points.

reasoning: Query asks for lesser-known UNESCO sites. Tool returns get_japan_heritage (Japan Heritage = domestic designation, NOT UNESCO World Heritage). The Asuka women story and Kamakura story are Japan Heritage, not UNESCO WHS — conflating these is a tool selection issue. Relevance_score is null throughout. Content is rich but 'lesser-known UNESCO' is not addressed. Japan Heritage is a valid nearby concept but the distinction matters.
missing_data: Actual lesser-known UNESCO WH sites: Hiraizumi, Tomisato Silk Mill, Amami/Okinawa islands (nature), Jomon Sites. These need separate tool or filter.
improvement_hint: Distinguish get_japan_heritage (domestic) from UNESCO WH sites; add a get_attractions filter for heritage_designations_labels='UNESCO World Heritage Site' to serve this query type.

reasoning: Query asks for areas where Jomon culture still influences daily life. Results include the Jomon Goddess statue (Yamagata), Jomon museum (Niigata), two Aomori museums near Sannai-Maruyama, and a Nagano article about Jomon experience workshops. Relevance is decent. The Nagano content (obsidian trade, Jomon living experience) directly addresses 'still influences daily life'. Missing the Tohoku/Hokkaido connection to the UNESCO Jomon Sites (2021).
missing_data: Sannai-Maruyama site (UNESCO 2021 Jomon), Oshoro shell mound (Hokkaido), Miharasaki area. Living tradition connections like Jomon-inspired crafts in Aomori.
improvement_hint: Tag Jomon UNESCO WH constituent sites with 'jomon' keyword; link Aomori Jomon museums to the UNESCO registration for better retrieval on culture-continuity queries.

reasoning: Chinese-language query asks about fermented food cultures across Japan. Tool returns get_local_food with 500/24K GI items in Chinese. Chinese translations appear accurate (青森黒加仑, 但马牛, 夕张甜瓜). However, fermented foods are not filtered for — returned items include cassis, beef, melon, gyokuro, pumpkin. The 'fermented food' constraint is completely ignored. No miso, sake, natto, narezushi, or tsukemono visible in truncated results.
missing_data: Fermented GI items: sake (nihonshu), miso, soy sauce (shoyu), narezushi, pickled vegetables by region. A keyword filter q='発酵' or q='醸造' would retrieve relevant items.
improvement_hint: Add keyword filtering support to get_local_food; for 発酵/fermentation queries, agent should use q parameter or call search_area(q='発酵食品').

reasoning: Query asks about hidden mountain villages with surviving shamanic traditions (山岳信仰/シャーマン). Tool returns get_traditional_arts listing UNESCO ICH items: Bunraku, Kabuki, Chakkirako, Dainichido Bugaku, Hayachine Kagura. Dainichido Bugaku (Akita) has mild shamanic/ritual elements. Hayachine Kagura too. But query specifically asks for 'mountain villages' — geographic placement missing. Bunraku/Kabuki are completely irrelevant urban arts.
missing_data: Specific shamanic traditions: Itako (Osorezan), Yamabushi (Dewa Sanzan), Noro in Okinawa, specific village names, visiting access.
improvement_hint: Search search_area(q='山岳信仰') or search_area(q='修験道') for mountain asceticism queries; get_traditional_arts without keyword filter returns all 183 items including irrelevant urban performing arts.

reasoning: Query asks for areas where atomic bomb history is embedded in daily landscape. Results are excellent: Hiroshima Peace Memorial (A-Dome, UNESCO WH, prominence 0.95), Children's Peace Monument, Cenotaph, Atomic Bomb Memorial Mound, Maruki Gallery (Saitama), Nagasaki Museum. Covers both Hiroshima and Nagasaki. Heritage labels and coordinates present. Kinds tags (monument, archaeological_site) relevant. Agent has strong material to answer.
missing_data: Nagasaki hypocenter park, Peace Park Nagasaki, Urakami Cathedral ruins. Description of how atomic bomb history integrates into daily life (survivor communities, school curriculum).
improvement_hint: Add Nagasaki-specific atomic bomb monuments to ensure geographic balance; both cities should appear with equal depth.

reasoning: Query asks for Japanese towns built around a single craft. get_local_specialty(category='craft') returns 231 METI-designated traditional crafts with production area data. Nibutani Attus (Ainu textile, Biratori-cho), Oitama Tsumugi (Yonezawa/Nagai/Shirataka), Ugo Shina Cloth (Tsuruoka/Murakami) — all exemplify single-craft towns. Production area text is specific. Descriptions are detailed and translated well. Strong response.
missing_data: Map context of production areas, visitor experience (workshops, studios open to public), seasonal availability.
improvement_hint: Add 'visitor_experience' field or link to DMO workshop booking pages for each traditional craft designation.

reasoning: Query asks where to see industrial decline preserved. Results include Tomioka Silk Mill (UNESCO WH, Gunma), Yawata Steel Works (Fukuoka), Nagoya industrial tourism portal, and DMO content mentioning Kosaka mining town (Akita) with multiple Important Cultural Properties and modern industry heritage. Mixed quality: Tomioka is excellent, Nagoya portal is broad, Ishikawa tourism guide is off-topic. 80 matches. Reasonable coverage.
missing_data: Hashima Island (Nagasaki), Sado Island gold mine, Miike coal mine. Specific visitor hours and access for preserved industrial sites.
improvement_hint: Add 'industrial_heritage' kind tag; filter out generic DMO homepage results that don't mention specific industrial sites.

reasoning: Korean-language query asks for region showing Japan's religious diversity best. get_japan_heritage(ko) returns 104 items with Korean translations. Asuka story (female emperors, Buddhism-Shinto fusion) and Kamakura story (Zen Buddhism, shrine-temple coexistence) are genuinely relevant to religious diversity. Korean translations appear fluent and correct. However relevance_score is null so no ranking by topic fit. Nara, Kyoto, Nagasaki (hidden Christians) would be ideal for religious diversity.
missing_data: Hidden Christian heritage (Nagasaki), Shugendo mountain practice, Ainu spirituality. A religion-themed query should surface Nagasaki or Ise.
improvement_hint: Implement theme-based filtering for get_japan_heritage (e.g., theme='religion') so agents can retrieve spirituality-focused stories; add relevance scoring.

reasoning: Query asks for dying/lost festivals in remote Japan. get_festivals returns 312 items (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property), all complete with names and shrine info. Kibune Matsuri (Kanagawa), Okumakabuto Festival (Ishikawa/Nanao), Kawagoe Hikawa Festival (Saitama, urban), Tatemon Festival (Toyama). Items are legitimate officially designated festivals, but most are not 'dying' or 'remote' — Kawagoe is a popular tourist town. No 'endangered' or 'low attendance' metadata. Cannot filter for remote/dying.
missing_data: 'Remote' flag or population density of host municipality; endangered status metadata. Festivals with <100 annual attendees vs. large-scale events.
improvement_hint: Add municipality population or remoteness flag; tag festivals with participation scale (community vs. major tourist event) to enable 'dying' festival queries.

reasoning: Query asks about coastal areas shaped by 2011 tsunami. Results are strong: Namie Town Ukedo Elementary School (Fukushima, earthquake remains), Kamaishi DMC disaster learning program, Iwate Tsunami Memorial (Rikuzentakata), Kesennuma disaster memorial museum (Miyagi), Minamisanriku reconstruction memorial park. Covers Fukushima, Iwate, Miyagi — the three main affected prefectures. DMO entries provide practical learning program context. Well-grounded.
missing_data: Visiting logistics: opening hours, tour booking for disaster learning programs. Memorial park design context (who rebuilt, Kengo Kuma's Minamisanriku design).
improvement_hint: Add opening hours and visitor program details to disaster memorial spots; link DMO learning programs directly to attraction records for richer agent context.

reasoning: Query asks for Meiji-era 擬洋風 (pseudo-Western) architecture. Tool returns search_area(q='擬洋風') with 70 matches but top results are completely irrelevant: Fukui tourism homepage in Chinese, Miyazaki tourism homepage in Chinese, Yamagata tourism in Traditional Chinese, Saga tourism intro. These are nav-chrome tourism portal pages that happen to contain the term somewhere. No actual 擬洋風 buildings visible.
missing_data: Named 擬洋風 buildings: Kaichi School (Matsumoto), Tohoku University old buildings, Yokohama/Kobe Western-style merchant houses, prefectural government buildings from Meiji era.
improvement_hint: Filter generic tourism portal homepages; add 'meiji_architecture' or 'pseudo_western' kind tag to relevant Wikidata and scrape entries; include coordinates for building-type attractions.

reasoning: Query asks for Japan's hidden Christian heritage. Tool calls get_japan_heritage(Nagasaki) returning 8 items. Top result is the Sugar Road (Nagasaki Kaido) — tangentially related as it covers trade routes that brought Christianity to Japan. However no direct 'hidden Christian' (隠れキリシタン) heritage story appears in the visible truncated output. The Goto Islands / Sotome hidden Christian communities would ideally appear.
missing_data: Japan Heritage story on hidden Christians (Nagasaki/Goto Islands), Wikidata entries for underground churches, Sotome church group (UNESCO WH).
improvement_hint: The Japan Heritage hidden Christian story should be in the Nagasaki dataset; verify story is indexed. Also call search_area(q='隠れキリシタン') in parallel to retrieve Wikidata church entries.

reasoning: Query asks for gardens by lesser-known masters. search_area(q='庭園') returns 81 matches. Hamarikyu (famous), Kyu-Shiba Rikyu (famous), Kiyosumi (famous Tokyo garden) — all well-known. Koke-mushiro in Ehime and Manpukuji garden in Shimane are more obscure. Tachihana-shi garden (Fukuoka) is interesting. No designer attribution is present in any record, so 'lesser-known garden masters' constraint cannot be addressed at all.
missing_data: Garden designer attribution; lesser-known gardens: Adachi Museum (Shimane, but famous), Rikugien (Tokyo), Kenroku-en alternatives. Designer biography links.
improvement_hint: Add garden designer attribution field to traditional garden records; without this metadata, 'by lesser-known masters' queries cannot be answered by any tool.

reasoning: Query asks where to experience traditional textile dyeing. get_local_specialty(category='craft') returns 231 METI craft items. Oitama Tsumugi (pre-dyed silk weaving, Yamagata) and Ugo Shina cloth are textile-related. However dyeing-specific items would be shibori, yuzen, aizome — these are presumably deeper in the 231-item list. Response is directionally correct (textiles = craft category) but dyeing is not surfaced in the visible portion.
missing_data: Dyeing-specific crafts: Kyoto Yuzen, indigo dyeing (Tokushima), Arimatsu-Narumi Shibori (Aichi). Workshop booking details for hands-on experience.
improvement_hint: Add keyword filter to get_local_specialty (q='染色' or q='染め') to surface dyeing crafts; top results are Ainu weaving and generic textiles which are less relevant to dyeing experience queries.

reasoning: Query asks for Edo-period highway (宿場) remnants. search_area(q='宿場') returns 90 matches with strong results: detailed Nagano Kiso Valley articles (Subarasuku, Nojiri-juku), Yanagimachi post town in Ueda with specific shop info and wheelchair access note, Kyumiteshuku Honjin (Wakayama, Important Cultural Property), Narai-juku (Nagano, Important Preservation District), and Tokaido post town feature from Shizuoka DMO. Excellent geographic and temporal specificity.
missing_data: Magome-juku, Tsumago-juku (most famous Kiso Valley examples). Practical info on preservation districts walking maps.
improvement_hint: Minor: ensure Magome and Tsumago are indexed with 'shukuba' tags; the results are already strong for this query type.

reasoning: Query asks for Japanese pilgrimages beyond Shikoku 88. get_japan_heritage(no filter) returns 104 stories. First two visible items are Asuka (ancient women/politics) and Kamakura (samurai history) — neither about pilgrimage. Kumano Kodo (UNESCO WH pilgrimage route), Dewa Sanzan, Togakushi, Saigoku 33 Kannon pilgrimage — all absent from visible output. Tool selection should have been search_area(q='巡礼') or get_japan_heritage with pilgrimage theme filter.
missing_data: Kumano Kodo, Dewa Sanzan, Saigoku 33 Kannon, Chichibu 34 Kannon, specific pilgrimage trail access and distance info.
improvement_hint: For pilgrimage queries, call search_area(q='巡礼') or get_japan_heritage with theme filter; unfiltered get_japan_heritage returns 104 items without relevance ranking, burying pilgrimage-specific stories.
